errors.correction: Leading = sentence structure pushes toward answer ('Don't you think/agree...'). Loaded = emotional adjectives ('excellent', 'reckless'). Different errors, different fixes.

errors.mistake: Missed implicit assumptions in 2 of 3 questions
errors.correction: Any question saying 'your last...', 'how often do you...', 'how much do you...' about a specific activity -- check if it assumes the respondent has done it. Fix with a filter question first.

errors.mistake: Said R² = r² (only true in simple regression)
errors.correction: R² = 1 - SSE/SST. In multiple regression, R² measures proportion of total variation explained by all X variables combined.

errors.mistake: Adjusted R² formula: used n-k instead of n-k-1
errors.correction: Adjusted R² = 1 - [SSE/(n-k-1)] / [SST/(n-1)]. Denominator is n-k-1 because you lose k slopes + 1 intercept = k+1 degrees of freedom.

errors.mistake: F-test: said SST/SSE instead of MSR/MSE; called MSR 'mean square residuals'
errors.correction: F = MSR/MSE. MSR = Mean Square Regression = SSR/k. MSE = Mean Square Error = SSE/(n-k-1). Not raw sums -- divide by degrees of freedom first.

errors.mistake: Said 'multiple dependent variables' instead of multiple independent variables
errors.correction: Multiple regression has ONE dependent variable (Y) and multiple independent/explanatory variables (X₁, X₂, ...). The 'multiple' refers to the predictors, not the outcome.
